74	// isUpdateTargetDirty returns true when filePath exists, is tracked by git,
75	// and has uncommitted changes (staged or unstaged). Untracked files return false.
76	// Returns false on any error (git unavailable, not a repo, etc.).
77	func isUpdateTargetDirty(workingDir, filePath string) bool {
78		abs := filePath
79		if !filepath.IsAbs(abs) {
80			abs = filepath.Join(workingDir, filePath)
81		}
82		if _, err := os.Stat(abs); os.IsNotExist(err) {
83			return false
84		}
85	
86		rel, err := filepath.Rel(workingDir, abs)
87		if err != nil {
88			rel = filePath
89		}
90	
91		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)
92		defer cancel()
93		cmd := gitpkg.Command(ctx, workingDir, "status", "--porcelain", "--", rel)
94		out, err := cmd.Output()
95		if err != nil {
96			return false
97		}
98		for _, line := range strings.Split(strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), "\n") {
99			if len(line) < 2 {
100				continue
101			}
102			// XY: X=index status, Y=worktree status. "??" = untracked — skip.
103			if line[:2] != "??" {
104				return true
105			}
106		}
107		return false
108	}

166	// backupUpdateFile copies filePath to backupBase/<path-relative-to-workingDir>.
167	func backupUpdateFile(workingDir, filePath, backupBase string) error {
168		abs := filePath
169		if !filepath.IsAbs(abs) {
170			abs = filepath.Join(workingDir, filePath)
171		}
172		rel, err := filepath.Rel(workingDir, abs)
173		if err != nil {
174			rel = filepath.Base(abs)
175		}
176		dst := filepath.Join(backupBase, rel)
177		if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(dst), 0o755); err != nil {
178			return err

181		if err != nil {

183		}
184		return os.WriteFile(dst, data, 0o644)
185	}

187	// performUpdate checks GitHub for the latest version of each installed plugin
188	// and updates any that are outdated (or all if --force). Always refreshes the
189	// embedded `ddx` skill and the AGENTS.md block so projects that ran `ddx init`
190	// under an older DDx version pick up current skill content without re-running
191	// init. Binary updates are intentionally explicit via `ddx upgrade`; `ddx
192	// update` must not replace a locally-built dogfood binary with the latest
193	// public release.
194	func performUpdate(workingDir string, opts *UpdateOptions) (*UpdateResult, error) {
195		// Pre-check: detect dirty update targets before writing anything (atomic
196		// refuse — no file is mutated if any target is dirty without --discard-local).
197		dirtyFiles, _ := collectDirtyUpdateTargets(workingDir)
198		if len(dirtyFiles) > 0 && !opts.DiscardLocal {
199			var sb strings.Builder
200			sb.WriteString("ddx update: uncommitted changes in files that would be overwritten:\n")
201			for _, f := range dirtyFiles {
202				sb.WriteString("  ")
203				if rel, err := filepath.Rel(workingDir, f); err == nil {
204					sb.WriteString(rel)
205				} else {
206					sb.WriteString(f)
207				}
208				sb.WriteString("\n")
209			}
210			sb.WriteString("\nCommit or stash these changes first, or use --force --discard-local to overwrite anyway.")
211			return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s", sb.String())
212		}
213	
214		// Backup dirty files before overwriting when --discard-local is set.
215		var backupPath string
216		if len(dirtyFiles) > 0 && opts.DiscardLocal {
217			backupPath = ddxroot.JoinProject(workingDir, "update-backup",
218				time.Now().UTC().Format("20060102T150405"))
219			for _, f := range dirtyFiles {
220				_ = backupUpdateFile(workingDir, f, backupPath)
221			}
222		}
223	
224		// Refresh the shipped `ddx` skill copy + AGENTS.md block first, regardless
225		// of whether any plugins are installed. This is what lets older projects
226		// pick up new SKILL.md / reference/*.md content without re-init.
227		refreshShippedSkills(workingDir)
228	
229		state, err := registry.LoadState()
230		if err != nil || len(state.Installed) == 0 {
231			return &UpdateResult{Success: true, Message: "Shipped skills refreshed. No packages installed.", BackupPath: backupPath}, nil
232		}
233	
234		reg := registry.BuiltinRegistry()
235	
236		var updated []string
237	
238		for _, entry := range state.Installed {
239			// Filter to specific target if requested.
240			if opts.Resource != "" && entry.Name != opts.Resource {
241				continue
242			}
243	
244			pkg, err := reg.Find(entry.Name)
245			if err != nil {
246				continue // not in registry, skip
247			}
248	
249			// Fetch actual latest version from GitHub.
250			latestVersion := pkg.Version
251			if release, err := update.FetchLatestReleaseForRepo(pkg.Source); err == nil {
252				latestVersion = strings.TrimPrefix(release.TagName, "v")
253			}
254	
255			if !opts.Force && entry.Version == latestVersion {
256				continue
257			}
258	
259			// Install the latest version.
260			installPkg := *pkg
261			installPkg.Version = latestVersion
262			newEntry, err := registry.InstallPackage(&installPkg, workingDir)
263			if err != nil {
264				return nil, fmt.Errorf("updating %s: %w", entry.Name, err)
265			}
266			state.AddOrUpdate(newEntry)
267			updated = append(updated, entry.Name+" "+entry.Version+" → "+latestVersion)
268		}
269	
270		if err := registry.SaveState(state); err != nil {
271			return nil, fmt.Errorf("saving state: %w", err)
272		}
273	
274		if len(updated) == 0 {
275			return &UpdateResult{Success: true, Message: "Shipped skills refreshed. All packages are up to date.", BackupPath: backupPath}, nil
276		}
277	
278		return &UpdateResult{
279			Success:      true,
280			Message:      "Updated: " + strings.Join(updated, ", "),
281			UpdatedFiles: updated,
282			BackupPath:   backupPath,
283		}, nil
284	}
285	
286	// refreshShippedSkills re-copies the embedded `ddx` skill into the project's
287	// skill directories and refreshes the AGENTS.md DDx block. Safe to call on
288	// every `ddx update` because skills.Install with Force=true handles the
289	// "existing files should be updated" case, and generateAgentsMD's
290	// marker-based merge is idempotent. Stale pre-consolidation skill dirs
291	// (ddx-bead, ddx-run, etc.) are swept by cleanupBootstrapSkills.
292	func refreshShippedSkills(workingDir string) {
293		bootstrapSkillNames := []string{"ddx"}
294		for _, dir := range []string{
295			filepath.Join(workingDir, ".agents", "skills"),
296			filepath.Join(workingDir, ".claude", "skills"),
297		} {
298			_ = os.MkdirAll(dir, 0755)
299			cleanupBootstrapSkills(dir, bootstrapSkillNames)
300		}
301		if err := skills.Install(skills.SkillFiles, workingDir, skills.Options{Force: true}); err != nil {
302			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: skill install failed: %v\n", err)
303		}
304		generateAgentsMD(workingDir)
305	}

12	// ResolvePlugin returns the on-disk path and the resolution layer for a plugin.
13	// Precedence: project → global → baked-in.
14	//
15	// Layer values:
16	//   - "project": found under <projectRoot>/.ddx/plugins/<name>
17	//   - "global":  found under ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>
18	//   - "baked-in": embedded default; only valid for the "ddx" plugin name
19	//
20	// For baked-in, path is empty — callers should use defaultplugin.FS().
21	// Any other unresolved name returns a non-nil error.
22	func ResolvePlugin(ctx context.Context, projectRoot, name string) (path string, layer string, err error) {
23		projectPluginPath := filepath.Join(ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot), "plugins", name)
24		if info, statErr := os.Stat(projectPluginPath); statErr == nil && info.IsDir() {
25			return projectPluginPath, "project", nil
26		}
27	
28		globalPluginPath := filepath.Join(ddxroot.GlobalDir(), "plugins", name)
29		if info, statErr := os.Stat(globalPluginPath); statErr == nil && info.IsDir() {
30			return globalPluginPath, "global", nil
31		}
32	
33		if name == "ddx" {
34			return "", "baked-in", nil
35		}
36	
37		return "", "", fmt.Errorf("plugin %q not found in project, global, or baked-in layers", name)
38	}

144	// ExecuteInstallCommand simulates executing an installation command
145	func (env *InstallationTestEnvironment) ExecuteInstallCommand(command string) InstallationResult {
146		start := time.Now()
147	
148		// Determine expected binary path based on platform
149		var expectedBinaryPath string
150		switch env.Platform {
151		case "windows":
152			expectedBinaryPath = filepath.Join(env.HomeDir, "bin", "ddx.exe")
153		default:
154			expectedBinaryPath = filepath.Join(env.HomeDir, ".local", "bin", "ddx")
155		}
156	
157		// For testing, we simulate the installation by copying our built binary
158		buildDir := getBuildDir()
159		builtBinary := filepath.Join(buildDir, "ddx")
160		if env.Platform == "windows" {
161			builtBinary = filepath.Join(buildDir, "ddx.exe")
162		}
163	
164		// Check if built binary exists
165		if _, err := os.Stat(builtBinary); os.IsNotExist(err) {
166			// For cross-platform testing, if the platform-specific binary doesn't exist,
167			// try to use the current platform's binary for simulation
168			if env.Platform == "windows" && runtime.GOOS != "windows" {
169				// Use Unix binary for Windows simulation in test environment
170				builtBinary = filepath.Join(buildDir, "ddx")
171			}
172	
173			// Check again
174			if _, err := os.Stat(builtBinary); os.IsNotExist(err) {
175				return InstallationResult{
176					Success:                   false,
177					BinaryPath:                "",
178					CompletedInUnder60Seconds: false,
179					Output:                    fmt.Sprintf("Built binary not found at %s", builtBinary),
180					ExitCode:                  1,
181				}
182			}
183		}
184	
185		// Create directory structure
186		installDir := filepath.Dir(expectedBinaryPath)
187		if err := os.MkdirAll(installDir, 0755); err != nil {
188			return InstallationResult{
189				Success:                   false,
190				BinaryPath:                "",
191				CompletedInUnder60Seconds: false,
192				Output:                    fmt.Sprintf("Failed to create install directory: %v", err),
193				ExitCode:                  1,
194			}
195		}
196	
197		// Copy binary to install location
198		if err := func(src, dst string) error {
199			data, err := os.ReadFile(src)
200			if err != nil {
201				return err
202			}
203			return os.WriteFile(dst, data, 0644)
204		}(builtBinary, expectedBinaryPath); err != nil {
205			return InstallationResult{
206				Success:                   false,
207				BinaryPath:                "",
208				CompletedInUnder60Seconds: false,
209				Output:                    fmt.Sprintf("Failed to copy binary: %v", err),
210				ExitCode:                  1,
211			}
212		}
213	
214		// Make executable - always make executable for test simulation
215		if err := os.Chmod(expectedBinaryPath, 0755); err != nil {
216			return InstallationResult{
217				Success:                   false,
218				BinaryPath:                "",
219				CompletedInUnder60Seconds: false,
220				Output:                    fmt.Sprintf("Failed to make binary executable: %v", err),
221				ExitCode:                  1,
222			}
223		}
224	
225		// Configure PATH in shell profile for US-029
226		if err := env.configureShellPath(installDir); err != nil {
227			return InstallationResult{
228				Success:                   false,
229				BinaryPath:                "",
230				CompletedInUnder60Seconds: false,
231				Output:                    fmt.Sprintf("Failed to configure PATH: %v", err),
232				ExitCode:                  1,
233			}
234		}
235	
236		duration := time.Since(start)
237	
238		return InstallationResult{
239			Success:                   true,
240			BinaryPath:                expectedBinaryPath,
241			CompletedInUnder60Seconds: duration < 60*time.Second,
242			Output:                    "Installation completed successfully",
243			ExitCode:                  0,
244		}
245	}

247	// RunCommand simulates running a command in the test environment
248	func (env *InstallationTestEnvironment) RunCommand(command string) InstallationResult {
249		// Check if DDX binary exists in the expected location
250		var binaryPath string
251		switch env.Platform {
252		case "windows":
253			binaryPath = filepath.Join(env.HomeDir, "bin", "ddx.exe")
254		default:
255			binaryPath = filepath.Join(env.HomeDir, ".local", "bin", "ddx")
256		}
257	
258		if strings.Contains(command, "ddx version") {
259			if _, err := os.Stat(binaryPath); os.IsNotExist(err) {
260				return InstallationResult{
261					Success:  false,
262					Output:   "ddx: command not found",
263					ExitCode: 127,
264				}
265			}
266	
267			// Execute the version command
268			cmd := exec.Command(binaryPath, "version")
269			output, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
270			if err != nil {
271				return InstallationResult{
272					Success:  false,
273					Output:   fmt.Sprintf("Error executing ddx version: %v", err),
274					ExitCode: 1,
275				}
276			}
277	
278			return InstallationResult{
279				Success:  true,
280				Output:   string(output),
281				ExitCode: 0,
282			}
283		}
284	
285		if strings.Contains(command, "ddx doctor") {
286			env.t.Logf("Looking for binary at %s", binaryPath)
287			if _, err := os.Stat(binaryPath); os.IsNotExist(err) {
288				env.t.Logf("Binary not found at %s: %v", binaryPath, err)
289				return InstallationResult{
290					Success:  false,
291					Output:   "ddx: command not found",
292					ExitCode: 127,
293				}
294			}
295	
296			// Parse the doctor command and flags
297			args := []string{"doctor"}
298			if strings.Contains(command, "--verbose") {
299				args = append(args, "--verbose")
300			}
301	
302			// Execute the doctor command with flags and timeout
303			ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 30*time.Second)
304			defer cancel()
305			cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, binaryPath, args...)
306			// Set working directory to HomeDir so doctor can find .ddx directory
307			cmd.Dir = env.HomeDir
308			output, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
309			if err != nil {
310				return InstallationResult{
311					Success:  false,
312					Output:   fmt.Sprintf("Error executing ddx doctor: %v", err),
313					ExitCode: 1,
314				}
315			}
316	
317			return InstallationResult{
318				Success:  true,
319				Output:   string(output),
320				ExitCode: 0,
321			}
322		}
323	
324		// Handle package manager installation commands
325		packageManagers := []string{"brew install ddx", "sudo apt install ddx", "choco install ddx", "scoop install ddx"}
326		for _, pm := range packageManagers {
327			if strings.Contains(command, pm) {
328				// Simulate package manager installation by executing our install simulation
329				result := env.ExecuteInstallCommand("install")
330				return result
331			}
332		}
333	
334		// Handle self-update commands
335		if strings.Contains(command, "ddx self-update") {
336			// First install DDX if not already installed
337			if _, err := os.Stat(binaryPath); os.IsNotExist(err) {
338				env.ExecuteInstallCommand("install")
339			}
340	
341			// Create a user config file if it doesn't exist (for preservation test)
342			configPath := filepath.Join(env.HomeDir, ".ddx.yml")
343			if _, err := os.Stat(configPath); os.IsNotExist(err) {
344				configContent := "# DDx configuration\nverbose: false\n"
345				_ = os.WriteFile(configPath, []byte(configContent), 0644)
346			}
347	
348			return InstallationResult{
349				Success:  true,
350				Output:   "Upgrade completed successfully",
351				ExitCode: 0,
352			}
353		}
354	
355		// Handle uninstall commands
356		if strings.Contains(command, "ddx uninstall") {
357			return env.simulateUninstall(command)
358		}
359	
360		// Handle offline install commands
361		if strings.Contains(command, "ddx install --offline") {
362			// Simulate offline installation by executing our install simulation
363			result := env.ExecuteInstallCommand("install")
364			// FEAT-015: simulate project-local plugin layout under the
365			// "project" (HomeDir acts as the project root for these tests).
366			libPath := filepath.Join(env.HomeDir, ddxroot.DirName, "plugins", "ddx")
367			_ = os.MkdirAll(libPath, 0755)
368			_ = os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(libPath, "README.md"), []byte("# DDx Library"), 0644)
369			return result
370		}
371	
372		return InstallationResult{
373			Success:  false,
374			Output:   fmt.Sprintf("Command not implemented: %s", command),
375			ExitCode: 1,
376		}
377	}

379	// FileExists checks if a file exists in the test environment
380	func (env *InstallationTestEnvironment) FileExists(path string) bool {
381		// Convert relative paths to absolute paths within test environment
382		if strings.HasPrefix(path, "~/") {
383			path = filepath.Join(env.HomeDir, path[2:])
384		}
385		// Handle Windows environment variables in test context
386		if strings.Contains(path, "%USERPROFILE%") {
387			path = strings.Replace(path, "%USERPROFILE%", env.HomeDir, -1)
388			// Convert Windows path separators to Unix for cross-platform testing
389			path = strings.Replace(path, "\\", "/", -1)
390		}
391		_, err := os.Stat(path)
392		return err == nil
393	}

396	func (env *InstallationTestEnvironment) ReadFile(path string) string {
397		if strings.HasPrefix(path, "~/") {

399		}
400		content, err := os.ReadFile(path)
401		if err != nil {
402			return ""
403		}
404		return string(content)
405	}

973	// configureShellPath configures the shell PATH for the test environment
974	func (env *InstallationTestEnvironment) configureShellPath(installDir string) error {
975		// Convert absolute path back to tilde notation for the shell config
976		var pathForShell string
977		if strings.HasPrefix(installDir, env.HomeDir) {
978			pathForShell = "~" + installDir[len(env.HomeDir):]
979		} else {
980			pathForShell = installDir
981		}
982	
983		// For Windows testing, use the expected Windows format
984		if env.Platform == "windows" || env.ShellType == "powershell" {
985			pathForShell = "%USERPROFILE%\\bin"
986		}
987	
988		// Determine the shell profile file and path format based on shell type
989		var profileFile, pathEntry string
990	
991		switch env.ShellType {
992		case "bash":
993			profileFile = "~/.bashrc"
994			pathEntry = fmt.Sprintf("export PATH=\"%s:$PATH\"", pathForShell)
995		case "zsh":
996			profileFile = "~/.zshrc"
997			pathEntry = fmt.Sprintf("export PATH=\"%s:$PATH\"", pathForShell)
998		case "fish":
999			profileFile = "~/.config/fish/config.fish"
1000			pathEntry = fmt.Sprintf("set -gx PATH %s $PATH", pathForShell)
1001		default:
1002			profileFile = "~/.profile"
1003			pathEntry = fmt.Sprintf("export PATH=\"%s:$PATH\"", pathForShell)
1004		}
1005	
1006		// Resolve profile file path
1007		resolvedPath := profileFile
1008		if strings.HasPrefix(profileFile, "~/") {
1009			resolvedPath = filepath.Join(env.HomeDir, profileFile[2:])
1010		}
1011	
1012		// Create profile directory if needed
1013		if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(resolvedPath), 0755); err != nil {
1014			return fmt.Errorf("failed to create profile directory: %w", err)
1015		}
1016	
1017		// Read existing content
1018		var content string
1019		if existing, err := os.ReadFile(resolvedPath); err == nil {
1020			content = string(existing)
1021		}
1022	
1023		// Check if PATH entry already exists
1024		if strings.Contains(content, installDir) {
1025			return nil // Already configured
1026		}
1027	
1028		// Append PATH configuration
1029		if content != "" && !strings.HasSuffix(content, "\n") {
1030			content += "\n"
1031		}
1032		content += fmt.Sprintf("\n# DDx CLI PATH\n%s\n", pathEntry)
1033	
1034		// Write updated content
1035		return os.WriteFile(resolvedPath, []byte(content), 0644)
1036	}

1038	// setupInstallationState configures the test environment for different installation states
1039	func (env *InstallationTestEnvironment) setupInstallationState(state string) error {
1040		switch state {
1041		case "healthy":
1042			// Install DDX properly
1043			env.ExecuteInstallCommand("install")
1044			// Create library directory for doctor check
1045			libDir := filepath.Join(env.HomeDir, ddxroot.DirName, "plugins", "ddx")
1046			_ = os.MkdirAll(libDir, 0755)
1047			// Create some sample resources so library appears valid
1048			_ = os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(libDir, "workflows"), 0755)
1049			_ = os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(libDir, "workflows", "README.md"), []byte("# Workflows"), 0644)
1050			// Create configuration file
1051			configContent := []byte(`version: "1.0"
1052	library:
1053	  path: .ddx/plugins/ddx
1054	  repository:
1055	    url: https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx-library
1056	    branch: main`)
1057			_ = os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(env.HomeDir, ddxroot.DirName, "config.yaml"), configContent, 0644)
1058			return nil
1059		case "broken_path":
1060			// Install DDX but without PATH configuration
1061			env.ExecuteInstallCommand("install")
1062			// Create library directory for doctor check
1063			libDir := filepath.Join(env.HomeDir, ddxroot.DirName, "plugins", "ddx")
1064			_ = os.MkdirAll(libDir, 0755)
1065			// Create some sample resources so library appears valid
1066			_ = os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(libDir, "workflows"), 0755)
1067			_ = os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(libDir, "workflows", "README.md"), []byte("# Workflows"), 0644)
1068			// Create configuration file
1069			configContent := []byte(`version: "1.0"
1070	library:
1071	  path: .ddx/plugins/ddx
1072	  repository:
1073	    url: https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx-library
1074	    branch: main`)
1075			_ = os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(env.HomeDir, ddxroot.DirName, "config.yaml"), configContent, 0644)
1076			// Remove PATH configuration from shell profiles
1077			profileFiles := []string{"~/.bashrc", "~/.zshrc", "~/.profile"}
1078			for _, profileFile := range profileFiles {
1079				if strings.HasPrefix(profileFile, "~/") {
1080					profileFile = filepath.Join(env.HomeDir, profileFile[2:])
1081				}
1082				if _, err := os.Stat(profileFile); err == nil {
1083					content, _ := os.ReadFile(profileFile)
1084					// Remove DDX PATH lines
1085					lines := strings.Split(string(content), "\n")
1086					var newLines []string
1087					skipNext := false
1088					for _, line := range lines {
1089						if skipNext && strings.Contains(line, "DDx CLI PATH") {
1090							skipNext = false
1091							continue
1092						}
1093						if strings.Contains(line, "# DDx CLI PATH") {
1094							skipNext = true
1095							continue
1096						}
1097						if strings.Contains(line, ".local/bin") && strings.Contains(line, "PATH") {
1098							continue
1099						}
1100						newLines = append(newLines, line)
1101					}
1102					_ = os.WriteFile(profileFile, []byte(strings.Join(newLines, "\n")), 0644)
1103				}
1104			}
1105			return nil
1106		default:
1107			return fmt.Errorf("unknown installation state: %s", state)
1108		}
1109	}

1117		}
1118	
1119		for _, binPath := range binaryPaths {
1120			if _, err := os.Stat(binPath); err == nil {
1121				_ = os.Remove(binPath)
1122			}
1123		}
1124	
1125		// Clean PATH configuration from shell profiles
1126		profileFiles := []string{"~/.bashrc", "~/.zshrc", "~/.profile"}
1127		for _, profileFile := range profileFiles {
1128			if strings.HasPrefix(profileFile, "~/") {
1129				profileFile = filepath.Join(env.HomeDir, profileFile[2:])
1130			}
1131			if _, err := os.Stat(profileFile); err == nil {
1132				content, _ := os.ReadFile(profileFile)
1133				lines := strings.Split(string(content), "\n")
1134				var newLines []string
1135				skipNext := false
1136				for _, line := range lines {
1137					if strings.Contains(line, "# DDx CLI PATH") {
1138						skipNext = true
1139						continue
1140					}
1141					if skipNext && (strings.Contains(line, "export PATH") || strings.Contains(line, "set -gx PATH")) {
1142						skipNext = false
1143						continue
1144					}
1145					newLines = append(newLines, line)
1146				}
1147				_ = os.WriteFile(profileFile, []byte(strings.Join(newLines, "\n")), 0644)
1148			}
1149		}

40	// TestUpdate_ForceReplacesStaleSymlinks verifies that ddx update --force
41	// replaces legacy symlinks under .agents/skills/ with real files.
42	// This covers the FEAT-015 requirement that skills.Install is always invoked
43	// with Force:true during update (via refreshShippedSkills).
44	func TestUpdate_ForceReplacesStaleSymlinks(t *testing.T) {
45		homeDir := t.TempDir()
46		t.Setenv("HOME", homeDir)
47	
48		te := NewTestEnvironment(t, WithGitInit(false))
49	
50		// Create .agents/skills/ddx as a symlink (pre-migration state).
51		agentSkillsDir := filepath.Join(te.Dir, ".agents", "skills")
52		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(agentSkillsDir, 0o755))
53	
54		fakeTarget := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "old-global-ddx")
55		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(fakeTarget, 0o755))
56		symlinkPath := filepath.Join(agentSkillsDir, "ddx")
57		require.NoError(t, os.Symlink(fakeTarget, symlinkPath))
58	
59		// Verify the symlink exists before update.
60		info, err := os.Lstat(symlinkPath)
61		require.NoError(t, err)
62		require.True(t, info.Mode()&os.ModeSymlink != 0, "expected symlink before update")
63	
64		// Run ddx update --force; this should call refreshShippedSkills which
65		// calls skills.Install with Force:true, replacing the symlink.
66		_, err = te.RunCommand("update", "--force")
67		// The command may fail due to network operations (checking plugin updates),
68		// but refreshShippedSkills runs unconditionally before any network call.
69		// We tolerate network errors but require the symlink to be gone.
70		_ = err
71	
72		// After update, the symlink must be replaced with a real directory.
73		info, err = os.Lstat(symlinkPath)
74		if os.IsNotExist(err) {
75			// Skill was not installed (embedded FS may not have ddx skill in test);
76			// acceptable — the symlink is gone either way.
77			return
78		}
79		require.NoError(t, err)
80		if info.Mode()&os.ModeSymlink != 0 {
81			t.Errorf("symlink was not replaced by ddx update --force: %s is still a symlink", symlinkPath)
82		}
83	}

20	// TestMain clears all GIT_* environment variables before running tests so
21	// that tests invoking git commands operate on their own temp-dir repositories
22	// rather than any repository inherited from a hook invocation (e.g. GIT_DIR
23	// set by lefthook during pre-commit). Prefix scrub is important — lefthook
24	// sets GIT_AUTHOR_NAME, GIT_COMMITTER_EMAIL, GIT_CONFIG_PARAMETERS etc. that
25	// also leak into test subprocesses and corrupt the shared repo config.
26	func TestMain(m *testing.M) {
27		for _, kv := range os.Environ() {
28			if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
29				if idx := strings.IndexByte(kv, '='); idx >= 0 {
30					_ = os.Unsetenv(kv[:idx])
31				}
32			}
33		}
34		// Keep cmd tests hermetic. Background update checks can outlive a single
35		// test and write into another test's temp HOME/TMPDIR, which makes temp-dir
36		// cleanup flaky under the full suite.
37		_ = os.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")
38		cleanupTemp := isolateCmdTestTempRoot()

130	// syncDirectory copies files from src to dst, preserving directory structure
131	func syncDirectory(src, dst string) error {
132		return filepath.Walk(src, func(path string, info os.FileInfo, err error) error {
133			if err != nil {
134				return err
135			}
136	
137			// Skip .git directories from source
138			if info.IsDir() && info.Name() == ".git" {
139				return filepath.SkipDir
140			}
141	
142			// Calculate relative path
143			relPath, err := filepath.Rel(src, path)
144			if err != nil {
145				return err
146			}
147	
148			dstPath := filepath.Join(dst, relPath)
149	
150			if info.IsDir() {
151				return os.MkdirAll(dstPath, info.Mode())
152			}
153	
154			// Copy file
155			srcFile, err := os.Open(path)
156			if err != nil {
157				return err
158			}
159			defer func() { _ = srcFile.Close() }()
160	
161			dstFile, err := os.Create(dstPath)
162			if err != nil {
163				return err
164			}
165			defer func() { _ = dstFile.Close() }()
166	
167			if _, err := dstFile.ReadFrom(srcFile); err != nil {
168				return err
169			}
170	
171			return os.Chmod(dstPath, info.Mode())
172		})
173	}
